rulings.Case Title: JEREMIAH M CADIGAN VS. TERRY W FONG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, December 17, 2010, line 1. DEFENDANTS TERRY FONG, BETSY FONG MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Continue to January 3, 2011. Opposing party to lodge courtesy copy of opposition per San Francisco Local Rule 2.6 B. Further, the register of actions indicates that plaintiff only filed the opposing points and authorities and nothing else. Plaintiff to provide filed endorsed copies of complete opposition to 302 and file all opposition immediately if necessary. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: PATRICK HELLAND VS. RAY BRONNER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Imposing Terminating Sanction Against Deft And Imposing Monetary Sanctions Agaisnt Deft For Falure To Obey Order Compelling Further Response
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, December 17, 2010, line 2. PLAINTIFF PATRICK HELLAND'S Motion For Order Imposing Terminating Sanction Against Defendant And Imposing Monetary Sanctions Agaisnt Defendant For Failure To Obey Order Compelling Further Response. GRANTED, NO OPPOSITION FILED. The answer is striken and default may be entered against defendant Ray Bronner. Plaintiff to calendar a prove-up hearing in the appropriate department. Defendant is also ordered to pay monetary sanctions to plaintiff in the amount of $1,750 within 30 days of entry of this order. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: JACOB BRISKMAN et al VS. TAGGED, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, December 17, 2010, line 5. DEFENDANT TAGGED, INC., GREG TSENG, JOHANN SCHLEIER-SMITH, LOUIS WILLACY, DEVIN DWORAK, DIEGO MATUTE, GERRY TANO DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT. Demurrer sustained without leave to amend as to all plaintiffs other than Rosenband, Briskman, Lynn, Phillip Rosenband and Shimberg. Third amended complaint fails to allege a benefit conferred as to those unnamed plaintiffs. No reasonable probability that defect can be cured by amendment. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: CLEANWELL COMPANY VS. JOHN B. LOGVY MANUFACTURING COMPANY, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice And Motion To Compel Deft John B. Logvy Manufacturing Ocmpany, Inc. To Provide Further Responses To Pltf'S Specially Prepared Interrogatories(Set One) And Request For Productin Of Documents And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, December 17, 2010, line 4. PLAINTIFF CLEANWELL COMPANY'S Motion To Compel Defendant John B. Logvy Manufacturing Company, Inc. To Provide Further Responses To Plaintiff's Specially Prepared Interrogatories (Set One) And Request For Production Of Documents And For Sanctions. Granted in part. Motion granted as it relates to the financial information of defendants except as to those items defendant claim are privileged or confidential. As to those items, defendants shall prepare and provide a privilege log pursuant to CCP 2031.240. Parties must meet and confer regarding disagreements in production and also discuss a protective order. Production and privilege log are due 20 days from service of this order. Motion denied as it relates to tax returns. Request for sanctions is denied, both parties acted with substantial justification. =(302/CWW)
